title: DAIDAI AJAZZ Diced Fruit Blueberry Keyboard Switch | 5 Pin 35gf Light Force Pre Lubed Linear Switch | Durable 80M MX Switch for Mechanical Keyboard (36PCS | Blueberry Switch)
features: ♫♫♫AJAZZ Diced Fruit blueberry switch for mechanical keyboard top cap and button part of the housing are made of transparent light blue and dark blue, blue-gray POM shaft core adds dust-proof wall, which increases the stability when pressing, and provides you with a smoother typing experience.

♫♫♫5 pin keyboard blueberry switches is the latest light pressure linear switch are made with operating force of 35gf and total travel of 3.8mm, 15.5mm double spring is more smooth when typing, it is perfectly compatible with 5pin hotswap mechanical keyboard.

♫♫♫Mechanical keyboard switch adopts MX structure design, which is widely compatible with most keycap sets with (X) crossbar, LED card slot and transparent PC material, which enhances the light transmission of keyboard LED lights, provide more choices and fun for fans who like personalized keyboard. (Note: Switch opener not included)

♫♫♫Prelubed linear switches in order to improve the smoothness and durability, it has been lubricated before leaving the factor, and the rebound is faster and smoother when the switch is pressed. The experimental test has exceeded 80 million keystrokes.

♫♫♫Friendly packaging design, open the packaging with a single switch, in order to experience the click feel to DIY enthusiasts, if you don't like it, you can return it, the remaining 35PCS switches are stored in transparent round cans, a bit like canned fruit, it is more convenient to store and carry it, perfect for gifting to yourself, friends or family.

♫♫♫AJAZZ fruit series custom switches in order to satisfy you who like DIY, we introduce different tactile or linear switches, whether beginners, professional gamers, typists and programmers can choose the most suitable AJAZZ fruit series switches. Create a unique typing experience for you.

reviews.title: Light, snappy, like a punchier Gat brown
reviews.text:
I previously got a tactile switch sampler set and honestly loved the AJAZZ Banana more than U4 Boba and its other iterations. I liked these more than Glorious Panda and Holy Panda. I type a lot as a student, so I didn't want anything too heavy--it gets tiring. I've been using AJAZZ Banana for two days so far now and I LOVE it!

I was using lubed Gateron browns before. I wanted something punchier but not too heavy. Banana is the perfect answer to that. Actuation force is about the same to Gat browns. I have a north-facing RGB board and the lights come through Banana switches beautifully.

In answer to the other reviewer, I believe that the Banana switches ARE lubed (with what, I don't know). I have a stock/un-lubed AJAZZ Banana switch from my switch sampler pack, these pre-lubed ones feel slightly lighter in comparison. Out of the box, these feel great without additional lubing. Results will vary depending on your mech keeb setup.

Note that the switch pack comes with 46 switches, so it won't be enough to do a full board. I have a 60% board and had enough to do the alphanumeric keys and other frequently pressed keys like Shift, Enter, Ctrl, Backspace, Alt, Tab, and punctuation keys. I left my Gat browns on the less frequent keys. I have two "extra" Banana switches as backups.

Highly recommend. If you want tactile but not heavy, this is a very affordable option.

reviews.title: Bananas are excellent tactile switch alternatives to browns and blues.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:dff950435419a7006b0295e50b846c44]] This review is for the Ajazz Diced Fruit Banana Switch.
I wanted to try these switches compared to a brown switch because of its similar characteristics.
These Banana switches are definitely tactile and smooth. They do have a tactile click sound and require a bit more force to actuate (not really noticeable for me because I'm heavy handed and I bottom out the key when I type). I'm not going to mention specs such as actuation force and distance because you can find that info in the product description or manufacturer's site.
I will talk about the "feeling" of these switches though because in everyday use, gaming and office work, I'm not thinking about the actuation force/distance, etc. For me it is how it feels and sounds.

So first off, these are excellent switches, no doubt. It is then just a matter of personal preference whether or not you like the characteristics of the Banana switch. For me, it is a bit too noisy. The tactile bump feels good but after using the Banana's for a while, I would hate typing while on mic because the mic picks up the keystrokes loud and clear and can be annoying for others to hear. I don't have blue switches to compare these Bananas to but I would imagine the blue switches would be worse in sound. I do have red switches to compare and the reds are noticeably quieter, I think mainly due to being linear (no tactile bump). For me and personal preference and usage, I would stick with the reds.

If you like tactile switches and are currently using a different tactile switch, I'd urge you give these Banana switches a try. See my video review where I installed these switches into a keyboard and compare to red switches, so you can get an idea of the sound when the switches are installed into a keyboard frame with keycaps on. In the video I have mistakenly called the red switches Gaterons, but I'm pretty sure they are Kailh reds with box stem.

reviews.title: Nice looking strong tactile
reviews.text:
I love tactile switches, so when this was offered for review I figured I'd give it a shot as I'd heard good things about Ajazz switches. It arrived and I swapped out the switches in my NK65 (DROP Holy Pandas) and threw these into the alphas.

In terms of feel they're a pretty strong tactile. I felt it important to mention that I was using HP's above because these feel very similar to those, just with a lighter spring. The bump is close to identical which is nice, and I prefer the lighter spring as I've been mainlining my Niz lately with 35g switches, so light is my thing right now. In terms of sound, the housing does make these quite a bit higher pitch than HP's, and I'd honestly put it in line with some Akko tactiles. Not too high to not enjoy, but higher than I'm initially used to.

Because these come pre-lubed they can be used stock, which is nice because while once I start I find lubing switches to be relaxing, but it's an activity I dread until I'm actually going. Just something about it seems beyond tedious, and to be fair, it is. Thankfully the lube job on these is pretty good and there's no ping to be heard or scratchiness to be felt. I did lube one just to see the difference that one done by hand can make, and the job on these is good enough that it makes no difference. The switch is fine as-is.

Other than being plate mount only I can't find a problem with these. The spring weight is nice and they sound and feel great to type on. I'm thinking about grabbing another set to build out a board with them in the future.

reviews.title: Kiwi switches
reviews.text:
This is a review for the Kiwi Switch from DAIDAI AJAZZ. The Kiwi switch is tactile, 50gf, and housed in glittery translucent green. If you have clear keycaps, you can see the yellow stem and a little of the upper housing.

For the Kiwi switches, you get 46 switches. For most keyboards, 46 switches is not enough and you will need 2-3 cans.

The switches come in a clear plastic can, with a screw lid and a pull tab. The packaging is cute! More importantly, the can takes up less space than the usual plastic tray, and it’s reusable.

I use Akko Vintage Whites (linear), but I wanted to try tactile switches for the thumb clusters on my ergo keyboard, so I had feedback when I pressed those keys. The tactile bump is smooth and pronounced, I definitely know I pressed that key.

I didn’t notice any difference between these pre-lubed Kiwi switches and non-lubed switches.

reviews.title: Pretty good tactile switches
reviews.text: I've tried linears and even clicky switches, but I really prefer tacticles. These are right in the sweet spot for me in terms of spring weight and tactile bump. Nice colors, if that matters to you. I got these to try out before buying a full set, and I like them quite a bit.

title: KEMOVE Maple Theme Keycaps, Thermal Sublimation PBT Keycap 70 Key Set, XDA Profile ANSI Layout for 60% Mechanical Keyboard, English (US) Layout-Orange White
features: ✅【60% keycaps】This Maple custom keycap set consists of 70 keycaps, compatible with 60% MX mechanical keyboards, applicable keyboard models: GANSS ALT61 series, RK61 series, IKBC poker series, Anne PRO, GH60, iqunix f60, GK64 GK61, etc., perfect for custom mechanical keyboard selection.
✅【XDA Profile Keycaps】XDA keycaps have larger surface contact and larger finger touch area with ergonomic stepped design. Long-term use is not easy to fatigue, with better comfort.

✅【PBT Heat Sublimation Keycaps】Compared with ABS, PBT has better high hardness, wear resistance and high temperature resistance. The color has penetrated into the keycap, and the service life is up to ten years without fading.

✅【Unique Design】This set of keycaps has a unique autumn maple leaves design. Autumn gives people a comfortable and peaceful atmosphere, making you feel calm when using the keyboard, improving work efficiency and increasing the winning rate of games.

✅【What You Get】1x70 PBT Keycap set (key puller included), Keycaps only, The keyboard in the picture is only for the demo and is not for sale. If you have any questions about the size of the keycap of your keyboard, please feel free to confirm with us.

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.text: Overall these are really nice keycaps that show off the light underneath well. However a few keys did have some small blemishes on the top side. The blemishes are hidden between the keys and only exists on the upper solid color portion.

reviews.title: Terrible QC, all keycaps had rough bumps
reviews.text:
I just got my white set in the mail today and they would be good keycaps if there had been better quality control. It's like they took a bunch of flawed pieces and sold them to us as a set. Like another user said, I expected the shift, caps lock and tab keys to have the words spelled out and not just arrows.

There are rough bumps and weirdly broken pieces of plastic at the top of every keycap, as you can see on my picture. It is very hard to read the keys with backlight on since the font is so thin. The keyboard photographs well but it's very hard to see what key you are pressing. I feel deceived by the company and wish I had bought the HyperX ones instead, even though I didn't like the font.

title: YUNZII AKKO CS Switch, 3 Pins for DIY MX Mechanical Keyboard, 45 pcs (Ocean Blue Switch)
features: 【About AKKO CS Switches】 AKKO Custom Series (CS) switches are the newly designed 3-pin switches for customers who have specific typing and gaming needs. Each package has 45 switches. Six types of switches are available and no matter which kind of typing feeling you want, we cover it all.

【Compatibility】AKKO CS switches are designed with a general MX structure so that they can fit most keycaps sets. The switch has 3 pins but also fit in all 3 pins and 5 pins keyboards. Based on the strong compatibility, you can easily change the switch and customize your mechanical keyboard.
【Built-in LED Slot 】The AKKO CS switches are equipped with a north-facing LED slot for LED modification. It is also compatible with SMD LED, provides more fun and options for keyboard enthusiasts.

【Durable Lifespan & Improved Performance】With a lifespan of approximately 50 million times of keystrokes, you are assured of high quality and a long lifespan. The CS switches feature a spiral spring design that can make the typing more comfortable.

【Ocean Blue Tactile Switch】The tactile bump of the Ocean Blue switch is at 0.55mm and it ends at 1.88mm, which gives long-continued feedback. Different from the blue and brown switch, the Ocean blue switch has a medium tactile position that can give you an excellent experience in typing and gaming.

reviews.title: Very good for the price
reviews.text: I'm not a mechanical keyboard connoisseur, but needed to replace the switches on my cheap Redragon keyboard to get rid of the loud clicking noise. The original switches were blue switch clones and the clicks will wake up the neighbors. These are cheap enough that I decided to give them a try. They are pretty good in terms of touch and sound, from my perspective and my use. Will definitely resort to these again in the future if I need to replace keyboard switches, especially for budget keyboards.

title: KINESIS USB-A Freestyle Pro Quiet Ergonomic Split Mechanical Keyboard (Cherry MX Silent Red Switches)
features: AWARD-WINNING SPLIT DESIGN: Advanced ergonomics for all body types and preferences via adjustable split, splay, and tent (tenting accessories sold separately). New cable storage compartment supports up to 20" of separation when you need it.

GENUINE CHERRY MECHANICAL SWITCHES FOR PREMIUM TYPING FEEL AND PERFORMANCE WITH LESS NOISE: Cherry MX "Silent" red mechanical switches feature a low activation force, linear feel and are rated for 50M key presses for unmatched durability.

FULL ONBOARD PROGRAMMABILITY WITH THE DRIVERLESS SMARTSET ENGINE: 9 custom layouts, 8 office hotkeys, on-the-fly macro recording, one-touch key remapping, pre-configured Mac and Dvorak layouts, settings saved to keyboards 4MB flash memory, and the SmartSet App GUI for Windows & Mac.
PLUG-AND-PLAY WITH ALL MAJOR OPERATING SYSTEMS: Windows 7-10, Mac OS X, Linux, Chrome.
2 YEAR MANUFACTURER'S WARRANTY: Buy with confidence knowing that Kinesis engineers all our products in the USA to the highest standards and stands behind them 100 percent since 1992.

reviews.title: Good, but could be better
reviews.text:
I use this with my MacBook Pro. I have brutal pain in my hands, wrists and forearms from osteoarthritis, tendon-patchy and tendonitis; caused from 19 years of being at my computer and holding my phone up for blogging.

This keyboard has made things so much better, however... I needed the keyboard to have the lift without the wrist pads which are agony for my hands. Only, the tent system is made to include all pieces; the lift and the wrist pads. So, I have to use the lift pieces without the wrist pads which leaves a chunk of awkward plastic sticking straight out from the keyboard; something I am constantly catching. It's frustrating to no end.

I am now looking for something I can simply glue to the inner, underside of the keyboard that will add the lift but leave the front open.

I'm also disappointed with the price. Come on now. It's sooooo overpriced. It's only a matter of time before another brand comes out with a more reasonable one- but in the meantime, I feel like they are taking advantage.

In the end, this is far better than nothing. It definitely places your hands in a more ergonomic position, and is better than a flat keyboard or laptop touchpad setup.

reviews.title: Great keyboard for ergonomics
reviews.text: The split keyboard layout has been great for my shoulders and arms.  It took a couple of weeks for me to get used to the layout but the extra functions offered on the edges of the keyboard are very helpful.  Being able to replace the key caps is huge.  I really love this keyboard.

reviews.title: Quality material, but as wide as TKL keyboards
reviews.text:
I wish the length of the cable was longer, and that a longer cable can be purchased separately.
For the price, the tenting should be standard, but it is a separate purchase. And, without them, this keyboard isn't as great.

I purchased the tenting separately, and it really helps with ergonomics when typing. But, when gaming it tends to push my hands side and down, so it's better to use a separate palm support.

I was surprised that when the keyboard is laid out, it is as wide as a ten keyless keyboard. I was hoping that it would be shorter, so that the mouse has more room. It's fine for work or just typing. For gaming, I'll put the right section farther up. Or, I'll put it above the keyboard tray, so I still have access to the keys when needed. That frees up a lot of room for the mouse.

The quality of the keyboard is great. Everything feels solid. The keys feel wonderful. And, the ergonomics is great for typing.
The tech support was friendly and helpful. I had a bit of a problem early on with customizing the keys, and their tech support promptly helped me.

reviews.title: Great keyboard
reviews.text: Nice quiet keyboard with a great feel.  I got the tenting and wrist rest kit.  It took a little bit to get used to the angle but I'm really enjoying it and have already programmed some of the keys to do routine things for me.

reviews.title: Perfect balance of ergonomics and key comfort.
reviews.text:
I have tried all the Kinesis keyboards with the exception of the 400$ console setup. I have chosen this one as the best overall experience, specifically paired with the VIP3Pro tent and wrist accessory. The keys on this keyboard have a much more consistent and pleasant feeling as compared to their membrane offerings as well as their silent switch option. Those keyboards are fantastic if you are not already addicted to the feeling of mechanical keys. However if you are searching for the ultimate in keyboard experiences for daily use, I heavily and without reservation recommend this keyboard set. The keyboard may initially seem expensive when compared to other ergonomic keyboards that do not split or offer the tenting/wrist rests, however having tried many different curved and non split ergonomic options from Microsoft, Logitech, and many others, I can say without a doubt that the optimal configuration requires the keyboard to be split. If you need to have a number pad like I do, I suggest any simple number key option, they'll fit nicely between the two halves and do not interfere at all. I plan to purchase a second keyboard for my WFH setup to compliment my work setup. I don't want to go back to a standard keyboard and you won't either after using it.

This review was not endorsed, sponsored, or influenced by any 3rd party, I am simply that overjoyed with the product.

title: SteelSeries PRISMCAPS - Double Shot Pudding-Style Keycaps - Durable PBT Thermoplastic - Compatible with a Wide Range of Mechanical Keyboards - White
description: Built for extreme durability with thermoplastic PBT keycaps and with enhanced RGB pudding-style lighting, PrismCaps include 2 additional sets of bottom row keycaps, making them compatible with the widest range of MX stem mechanical keyboards.
features: Ultra-wide compatibility with both standard and non-standard bottom row keycap sets to fit most mechanical keyboard brands, including SteelSeries, Razer, Corsair, and virtually all other MX stem mechanical keyboards
Standard US ANSI 104 keys works on full sized, tenkeyless, and 60% mini keyboard layouts
Enhanced lighting displays brilliant pudding-style RGB illumination through a specially engineered double shot design with translucent sides and letters
Retains consistent texture and appearance even after heavy use with highly durable double shot PBT keycaps
Included keycap puller is built with finger hole grip to pull any keycap out with ease and flexible enough to fit around any standard keycap.

reviews.title: for apex pro
reviews.text: bought these for my apex pro. they fit perfect and i definitely like them better than the caps that came with the keyboard

reviews.title: Great keys for $25
reviews.text: I really like these keys because it is very nicely built. The plastic is thick and there is some texture. The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. I’d still recommend this for putty caps.

reviews.title: Great keycaps
reviews.text: i got these for my Apex Pro they look amazing on it about the only thing about them is more about the apex then the keycaps,the space bar only has one led on the keyboard in the center so does not light up the spacebar as much as the rest of the keys and is pretty noticeable...the kit came with everything needed to be used on several different keyboards that i have so i might get another set for my corsair boards next.

reviews.title: They look so good!
reviews.text: Made my Steelseries Apex 7 keyboard pop even more. They feel great with my Red Linear Switches. They are a little more expensive than some other brands, but I feel like it was better to go with the same brand since I have seen other people have issues with switching out the keycaps on Steelseries keyboards.

reviews.title: Love these!
reviews.text: Amazing PBT pudding keycaps, I absolutely love these! I chose these to match my SteelSeries keyboard and I'm very happy with them.

title: Redragon K556 RGB Mechanical Gaming Keyboard M612 Wired Gaming Mouse Bundle
features: This bundle includes a K556 gaming keyboard and a M612 Mouse.

K556 Keyboard: The mechanical brown switch offers the best tactile typing experience, also being ever so slightly quieter, fitting for office or gaming use. And you will get 8 spared switches as a gift.

K556 Keyboard: RGB LED adjustable lighting with 6-themed backlights -18 lighting models. You can also adjust their brightness with FN+“↑”and“↓” Besides, there is a 9-color single backlit mode for this keyboard.

M612 Mouse: Pentakill, 5 DPI Levels - Geared with 5 redefinable DPI levels (default as: 500/1000/2000/3000/4000), easy to switch between different game needs. Dedicated demand of DPI options between 500-8000 is also available to be processed by software.

M612 Mouse: Any Button is Reassignable - 11 programmable buttons are all editable with customizable tactical keybinds in whatever game or work you are engaging. 1 rapid fire + 2 side macro buttons offer you a better gaming and working experience.

reviews.title: Small kickstands
reviews.text: The kickstands or keyboard legs are short so even when they are up, keyboard still feels pretty small. Other than that, really good keyboard.

title: dagaladoo XVX 131 Keys Double Shot Keycaps, Cherry Profile PBT Keycaps Full Set, Custom Keyboard Keycaps for 60% 65% 75% 100% Cherry Gateron MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard, Dark Blue/Black/White
features: Full 131 keys custom keycaps set, including 108 standard keys and 23 modifiers keys, suitable for most ASIN US and UK layout 61/64/66/68/84/87/96/98/100/104/108 mechanical keyboards.
Cherry profile keycaps, ergonomic shapes design and curved layout, provide a lasting and comfortable experience of typing to you.
Double shot design keycaps, made from two colors material molded into each others, make the keycaps characters maintain the vibrance and saturation, clear and not fade.
Made from high content PBT material, 1.3mm extra thick keycaps wall, which is provide top-notch durability, excellent touch and grease resistance.
Minimalist theme style and classic color matching keycaps, will be a powerful addition to for personalized of your keyboards.

reviews.title: Great budget keycaps
reviews.text: Please don’t mind my cat lol she’s modeling the keyboard. Really great budget keycaps, super pretty color. I use aqua king switches and the sound with the keycaps is pretty good. Really like and enjoy them. P.S. the yellow keycaps ARE NOT part of the set, they are from another keycap set. I thought it would compliment the purple nicely :)

reviews.title: Nice keycaps, but holy crap they're snug
reviews.text:
These keycaps are probably best suited for soldered switches. If you're on a hot swap board, get ready to separate every key from the cap as you'll be pulling the entire assembly out EVERY TIME.

Other than my tolerance/keycap ripping issues these are solid and have a good sound indicative of a good pbt material.

reviews.title: Great keycap set, just wish they we're backlight compatible.
reviews.text: That's my fault, I always assume keycaps are backlight compatible but they feel awesome, love the satin touch, they look great, and the legend is super bright and clear. If you're not specifically looking for a keycap set to use with a backlit keyboard, this one looks great.

title: HONKID Esc Keycap for Mechanical Gaming Keyboard Switches, Custom Keycaps for Esc and Tab Key with Magnetic Light Macintosh Retro Style Design (White)
features: TRIBUTE TO THE CLASSICS: The retro computer keycaps is in honor of the the classic 1984 Macintosh computers, the unique retro styling of ESC keycap and Tab keycap give you a new experience

COMPATIBILITY: Uinversal mechanical keyboard Cross interface, compatible with most MX switches as cherry MX, kailh, outemu, gateron, ect. Our custom keycaps fit most keyboards especially 60% mechanical gaming keyboard, the keycaps include ESC keycap and Tab keycap

LIGHT-TRANSMISSION DESIGN: Unique light guide design, the light transmission is brighter and more uniform. If your keyboard has a backlight function, then your light will be delivered through the keycap card, and the effect is perfect. Five classic card patterns, which can be changed at will

MAGNETIC DESIGN: Both ESC and Tab keycap have magnetic, simple and stable.You can attach the mini keyboard to any keycap, card panel of the keycap also uses the magnetic function, which makes it very convenient and fast to replace the translucent cards
DELICATE EXQUISITE: You will feels better and more delicate and comfortable,The delicate matte texture and the details of the back are enough to make you miss the first generation of MAC

reviews.title: Perfect starter Artisan keycap!
reviews.text:
Edit: purchased the black AND the white one. 10/10

I purchased this keycap as I wanted an artisan but did not want to have to pay resale on ebay or Mechmarket. After a quick search, I was glad to find this combo as it covers the escape key AND the tab key in a matching set. I tried both keycaps on multiple boards and I am thoroughly impressed.

I can easily say that this keycap set will fit both regular MX stems and box stems, as they fit on my box inks and NK creams without issue. I swapped them on 6 different boards just to see how they looked with different sets and did not find there to be any loosening.

Being able to change the "screen" is a fun touch but I am leaving it on the apple logo for nostalgia. The keyboard on the tab key is magnetic and I have found myself playing with it while working today.

Overall this is an easy 10/10 for both price and quality. I highly recommend it for both enthusiasts who want a fun budget set but also for anyone with a keyboard in general!

title: YMDK GH60 64 Minila PCB Type C Fully Programmable QMK VIA for DIY Mechanical Keyboard Poker Faceu HHKB Support LED (Black QMK No Underglow)
description: 1.This is the link only for keyboard PCB board,no keyboard included.It's flashed with firmware already and support LED adding.It supports both ANSI and ISO layout. 2. Black gold pad PCB is QMK PCB which supports the layout in image as well as Backspace split to 2x1u Lshift split to 1.25u+1u Rshift split to 1.75u+1u 3. Manual Black PCB QMK drive.google.com/file/d/1mH55MzYKdZa5G7SBaFoLjncoDW5Hdz3V/view?usp=sharing Black PCB VIA manualsdrive.google.com/file/d/1iHBv3uwBLPdGaUBeAcrGn9RgJLEhqn0k/view?usp=sharing
features: Package included: 1x Black QMK Type C Connector PCB,no underglow RGB
All resistors chipset is presoldered.However it's not hotswap,you need solder switches on yourself
It supports most 61 64 Minila layout.Can see the image for all supported layout
QMK PCB is programmed with QMK firmware.Can find manuals link in detail page
New edition support all 61 64 layout as the image show,you need to use the plate which support these layout at the same time to support the layout you prefer.

reviews.title: !!!!!Great PCB!!!!!!!!
reviews.text:
Used this for my Switch Couture Electrotype60 amazing PCB and pretty easy to solder, especially for an amateur solderer like me, definitely will be buying another one when I have another case to build. Fit perfectly, No problems soldering, and definitely came out perfect out of the box, though I don't know how to brighten up the LED nor change the modes, I'm completely fine with the RGB cycle. I'm Typing on my new keyboard as we speak and it works perfectly, no delays, no nothing, just perfect!!!!

(I can admit I'm kinda too last to do VIA so I'm just working with it lol)

title: 66 Pieces Gateron Yellow Switches for Mechanical Keyboards,3-pin KS-9 Supporting SMD RGB Light | Linear | 50g Force
description:
Specifications：
Gateron Red Switch（Office）---Linear---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force45±15gf---Bottom out force60±15gfGateron Brown Switch（Universal axis）---Tactilre&Clicky---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force55±15gf---Bottom out force70±15gfGateron Blue Switch（Typing artifact）---Tactilre&Clicky---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.3±0.6mm---Actuation force60±15gf---Bottom out force70±15gfGateron Yellow Switch（Smooth）---Linear---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force50±15gf---Bottom out force65±15gfGateron Black Switch（Game）---Linear---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force60±15gf---Bottom out force75±15gfGateron White Switch（Light）---Linear---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force35±15gf---Bottom out force50±15gfGateron Green Switch（Potent）---Tactilre&Clicky---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.3±0.6mm---Actuation force70±15gf---Bottom out force80±15gf
Note：
During the delivery process, the pins of the switch may touch and bend. This is inevitable. Bent pins can be straightened with nail clippers, which will not affect normal use.
features: Switches Compatibility：Gateron KS-9 RGB Mechanical MX Type Key Switch-Clear top，Supports two-pin 3mm round lights, four-pin RGB lights and patch RGB lights.Compatible with Cherry MX switch, suitable for most mechanical keyboards.
Main Parameters：Gateron Yellow Switch---Linear feel (straight up and down), Actuation force is about 50±15gf.Over 50 million laboratory tests ensure durability and more stable and reliable quality.

Suitable For：Gateron Yellow Switch---The yellow switch feels smooth, rebounds faster, and feels stronger when pressed. You can feel it making the keycap follow your fingers more closely. The yellow switch is our recommended switch for those who feel that the red switch is too light, too soft, and needs a little stronger feedback.
Installation Type:Plate mounted switches (3 pins)
Package Included：1 puller tool and 66 Yellow Switches ‘+’ shape and PP protection box.

reviews.title: Almost perfect :)
reviews.text: A few had slightly bent pins,  but safely fixed with tweezers. Crisp clicking on the Blues. Would recommend if asked.

title: Akko Keycaps Set, Black&Bronze Theme 158-Key ASA Profile PBT Double-Shot Keycap Set with Black Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards with Collection Box
features: BLACK&BRONZE THEME – The combination with classic black, gray and dark bronze,　the Black&Bronze keycaps brings heavy sense of history. The bronze charactors shine on the dark keycaps let us fantasy the bronze gleams in the dark night through thick fog.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the 158-key keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including but not limited to 61-key(60%), 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%) and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure switches including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh etc. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Some of the best keycaps under $100
reviews.text:
These keycaps are fantastic. The quality of material is noticeable right when you open the package, and the layout compatibility is uncomparable. For about $60 you get keycaps that work with the following layouts:

Full Size
1800
96%
75%
65%
60%
40% (non ortholinear)

The only layouts that aren't supported are ortholinear (though you might be able to make something work) and split (no split spacebar here). 60% with arrows (the 64-key) also is not supported as there is no 2u shift.

The colors are fantastic, the material feels very nice, and the cap profile is just incredibly comfortable to type on. In fact, it's so comfortable the only caps that I think are nicer are Drop's MT3 which cost double and don't come with as much layout variation without buying extra kits.

This specific set, Dracula's Castle, also comes with custom novelites and two different colors worth of compatibility (crimson and black) for a lot of the keys to make your board look exactly the way you want.

I'd highly recommend anything in ASA profile. The Cherry profile is good too, but not s comfortable for me to type on personally.

reviews.title: So amazing for the price
reviews.text: They looks so good for the price, Akko does it again!

reviews.title: Great quality double shot pbt keycaps.
Pretty Great Set with an Interesting Profile
reviews.text: This set is pretty nice, unlike most of Akko's keycap sets this set doesn't directly rip off color ways from existing GMK or EBPT sets, it comes close to something like SA Bliss but it adds black mods so it's not exactly the same.  The keycaps themselves are fairly nice and the font of the legends are a lot more consistent than their cherry profile sets. The packing is nice albeit a little frustrating as you pretty much need to use a keycap puller to remove the keycaps. The ASA profile is what I found the most interesting, if I had to compare it to anything I would actually compare it to OEM profile but slightly taller with rounded edges than something SA profile, The sculpting is a lot more derivative of OEM than SA in my opinion. The typing feel is pretty great it is very reminiscent of OEM profile so if you are used to OEM profile you will be right at home. The sound is more clacky than thocky at least in my NK65 with Gateron Cap Gold Switches. Overall the keycaps are nice and the ASA profile is a pleasant surprise coming from someone that uses both SA and OEM profile fairly regularly.

reviews.title: Good quality, Vivid colors, Nice sounding, Great value
reviews.text:
Akko Los Angeles 158-Key ASA Profile PBT Double-Shot Full Keycap Set for Mechanical Keyboards with Collection Box

I really love the profile of these keycaps. I have a hard time with whether to call this company a knock off company or not. They do have their own color combos and make combos that other companies do for a lot cheaper. Are color combos and fonts really proprietary? I'm not sure and eventually there will be no more combination of colors that one company will be able to claim as only their colors. I do think that Akko make really good keycaps and you get a lot for your money. So that's my two cents.

title: nuphy Air60 Portable Wireless Keyboard, 60% Low Profile Mechanical Keyboard, Supports Bluetooth 5.0, 2.4G and Wired Connection, Compatible with Windows and Mac OS Systems-Gateron Brown Switch
features: ✨【The new upgrade】：NuType F1 is highly praised by many users. On this basis, we have upgraded NuType F1 in all aspects and got the latest Air60 low-shaft mechanical keyboard. It adopts 60% layout (64 keys), bright color scheme, and the design of low shaft will not hurt your wrist after using the keyboard for a long time. The wide keycap design can help reduce the number of keystroke errors.

💻【Three-mode design】：Each user's usage is different. Based on user needs, we try to adapt the Air60 to as many users as possible, so the Air60 keyboard supports 2.4G, Bluetooth wireless connection and type C wired connection, and can connect up to 4 equipments at the same time to meet more complex user needs.

💪【Alternative laptop keyboard】：The four foot pads on the back of the Air60 can be perfectly embedded between the keys of the notebook keyboard. Typing on the Air60 will not touch the notebook keyboard at the bottom, giving notebook users a better typing experience. It is compatible with most laptops on the market, including the latest Macbooks.

💡【60% Portable Keyboard】：Compared with Air75, Air60 has no F area and will be smaller in size. If you don’t use F area often, Air60 is undoubtedly a better choice for use on the go; if you often use a laptop, Air60 can be perfectly placed. On the keyboard of the notebook computer, it is currently suitable for the vast majority of computers on the market, especially the Mac series, and it is more convenient to carry.

🔋【2500mAh battery】：The battery size of the Air60 wireless keyboard is 2500 mAh. The laboratory test time is more than 48 hours, which can meet the needs of a week of office work. When the backlight mode is turned off, the use time can be greatly increased.

reviews.title: Works great. Battery life is pathetic
reviews.text: Keyboard works great. Compact. Keys are responsible with good travel. The battery, however, lasts just about a day, which is unacceptable.

reviews.title: Didn't think low profile could feel this good...
reviews.text:
I was hesitant to try out this keyboard because I have always avoided low profile mechanical keyboards.

Maybe because I did actually type very well and fast with the chic-lit style keyboards that this keyboard reminds me of and I am too deep into the mechanical keyboard hobby to really want to move to low profile.

I am glad I took the leap!

I really like the way the browns feel on this keyboard. It is a pleasure to type on and the space bars and other stabilized keys feel great. Out of the box I don't think there is anything you need to do to this keyboard except use it!

Some things will take getting use to like the smaller shift button. I actually wouldn't mind if this board was spread out a bit more to allow for more traditional length buttons, but I'm sure it is something I could pick up quickly.

The build quality feels excellent and the polished steel plate on the bottom is a great touch. It really looks classy and I love the look of this keyboard. Nuphy's color-ways have always been super slick to me and this is no different.

The board has awesome RGB effects that you can change easily from the board itself. It connects wired, through bluetooth, or through a 2.4ghz adapter. Bluetooth connected instantly and works super well.

It comes with a ton of accessories, from magnetic feet to tilt the board a bit to extra keycaps, pullers, switches, stickers, and the instructions are also a poster of an anime chick which would probably look good in a gamer style room.

I am really tempted to try and use this board as a daily driver but right now I'm using it with it sitting on my M1 Macbook Airs keyboard and it is working great for that use case. It is thin enough that it doesn't block the screen or anything like that.

I have another compact 60% board that has the same goals as this Nuphy board and it misses the mark. Nuphy really hit the nail on the head with this board.

title: Lomiluskr K680T Wireless 60% Mechanical Keyboard, Ultra-Compact Bluetooth/Wired Dual Mode Keyboard, White Backlight, Compatible for Multi-Device Connection (Black Switch, Black)
features: 【68 Key Mini and Compact Layout】60% Layout 68Keys Mechanical Keyboard. As a compact mechanical keyboard, while minimize the size to 13.4×4.25×1.4inch, we maintain the Mac key position and commonly used keys for games. This size saves lots of desktop space, giving your right hand and mouse more space, whether you are working, gaming or daily use, it will perform perfectly. Small in the appearance but big inside

【Bluetooth Wireless/Wired Mode】Designed with Bluetooth mode and wired dual mode, you have more choices. Bluetooth3.0 is stable and strong enough to ensure 10 meters transmission distance. It can connect up to 3 devices at the same time, simply press FN+Q/W/E to exchange between devices, easy to use and suitable for both work and game. Moreover, the wired mode will also charge the keyboard at the same time, a cool function and time-saver indeed

【1000mAh Built-in Large Capacity Battery】N-Key Rollover and Built-in 1000mAh Battery. This model is programmed to be N-key rollover in wired mode. The keys won’t conflict with each other. Save you from trouble while playing a game. Built-in large capacity battery ensures long time use without falling behind

【18 Light Effects】The letters are made to shine-through if the backlight is on. Simple backlight but you have got up to 18 light effects, classic and neat (flashing, static or breathing, etc.) use FN+→/←/↑/↓ to choose the ideal mode, freely adjust the brightness and light speed.

【Professional Mechanical Switches】Mechanical Switches and Professional Chip. This model is equipped with the custom chip for fast and lossless response. The 68 keys are using the real mechanical switches for ultimate tapping experience. Lightweight, weak tactile and audible feedback, these switches are the perfect combination of typing and gaming, more than 50 million click tests for excellent durability.

reviews.title: Won't pair with M1 Macbook Air, works well with Dell XPS 9370 and Acer Triton Predator
reviews.text:
For windows users this keyboard is great. The brown switches feel somewhat similar to higher end cherry brown switches and I had no complaints with the build quality. There are various lighting modes (my favorite is the single keystroke light trail).

I was slightly disappointed with the Bluetooth not working on my M1 Macbook Air but I had no other issues with various windows laptops. There is some latency noticeable when waking from sleep but it's not too big of a deal. For gamers it might be though so you'll want to use it wired.

Overall it's a good deal for a mechanical 60% bt keyboard as long as you're not expecting premium switches or bt 5.0 connectivity.

reviews.title: Compact But Has Good Weight, Keys Are Perfect
reviews.text:
I opted for the "brown" keys and I must say that they feel and respond perfectly. Just the right amount of "clack" and just the right amount of resistance. The keys are normal width but the footprint is compact so you don't have to sacrifice any typing comfort for the portability or the ability to work in small spaces. The keyboard itself is very weighty and between its heft and rubber feet it's pleasingly very stable - won't slide around on the desk. The ability to use either USB or Bluetooth makes this thing incredibly versatile. Thankfully it's USB-C so you don't have to worry about plug alignment, and the included USB cable is very good quality (braided nylon, strain relief) and length. The backlighting is decent, though not terribly even so some of the lettering doesn't look right in the dark.

One big downside is the lack of dedicated Function keys (F1-F12). This keyboard takes a cue from laptop keyboard design and introduces an "Fn" key which you have to hold in to change the function of normal keys to specialty operations, but instead of controlling volume or screen brightness, the "Fn" key is required to activate bluetooth pairing, an feature that acts sort of like Caps Lock but for function keys and of course the "F" keys. The problem I ran into with this configuration is that the F1-F12 keys weren't being recognized with some devices. I actually bought this specifically to use with a MiSTer FPGA device which supports standard USB keyboards and requires the F keys for certain basic functions.

According to the instructions, the combination "Fn + 1" should look like an "F1" key press but it did not. After poking at it for some time I discovered an undocumented (as in it's not in the instructions anywhere) feature which subtly changes the keyboard's operation. The "A" and "S" keys have an additional label "Os1" and "Os2" respectively. Holding "Fn + A" or "Fn + S" makes the keyboard backlight flash (which it does whenever you've changed some function of the keyboard itself). With the keyboard set to "Os2" (Fn + S) the "Fn + 1" combination DOES send "F1" but, this arrangement also confusingly swaps the "Window" key and the left "Alt" key.

Bottom Line ---
Pros:
- Heavy
- Quality keys, great feel
- Bluetooth & USB
- USB is USB-C and comes with a good cord
- Easy to plug in and use

Neutral Observations:
- Backlight is uneven

Cons:
- Instructions are incomplete
- Not having dedicated F1-F12 keys is problematic for gaming

reviews.title: OMG great keycap set!
reviews.text:
I have been looking for these sets for the longest time, outside of GB and these guys have made a solid replica. i am so impressed with the quality and feel of these Botanical keycap sets that the premium sets can remain on the shelf. They look so nice and very very close and clean to the originals.

the only way you can tell that they are not GMK is by looking at the bottom. The dye-sub is a little weak in the finish but you never look under the hood.

If you like the greenery landscape or the color great fanatic this is the best set. It's super clean, subtle, and sounds great! I have this set on my KBD67 Lite and it sounds so much better than my ABS keycaps i paid $80 for. The texture is also very nice and I seem to be keen on this rough like texture than the ABS smoother feel. It kind of is matte if that makes sense. The legends on all the alphas are uniform, no thickness issues. The ,.? and other modifiers might have a thinner font but negligible.

I can't say enough what a great first impression these sets have given me and if they produce an "Oblivian" replica i'm jumping on it as well. Pictures to come, after I pickup my desk from all the loose keycaps.

reviews.title: Where's the documentation?
reviews.text: Very heavy keyboard. Certainly doesn't feel like a mechanical keyboard. The tiny insert offers ZERO CLUES as to how the keyboard is supposed to function, ie there is no documentation. The web site reads more like an ad, no clues there, either. The flashing color LEDs remind me of a 1980s video arcade game. You can reduce the brightness down to zero but that's about it. There's a really ugly and difficult to read quasi-sci-fi typeface used on the key caps. The keyboard itself is quite nice, quite heavy. Little kickstand-type feet if you want it angled. A small plastic ramp is included if  you want to rest  your left hand. What's missing here is a MANUAL of some kind. Even a PDF would be just great. Otherwise it's a mystery how this keyboard is supposed to work, what the features are. Somebody blew it. It's nicely designed with some interesting features, but unless you enjoy hardware sleuthing, how are you supposed to know what works, and how it works? Rather maddening. So I don't see this being used, I'll go back to my older keyboard which actually has mechanical clickity click key responses. It's baffling that with  the attention to detail paid to this keyboard that they wouldn't include or provide some DOCUMENTATION. It's basic.

reviews.title: This is an amazing keyboard! With this and the mouse, I am ecstatic! Don't hesitate on this! Can I give it 6 stars?
reviews.text:
Oh wow! I have waited for several days to review this because my computer was broken and I had to wait for a service tech to drive down and DO something, ANYTHING, with it. When the box came, I was asking myself what it was because the box was HUGE, The inner box was still pretty big, but not huge, It provides a lot of information--including that the keyboard is waterproof, nice since I'm a klutz--and something I'd either not noticed or had forgotten. In side that was a plain black box with a Bloody hand--or is it a hand print? The lights are cool, and the bloody hand lights up on both the keyboard and the Bloody Mouse I got a couple of days before the keyboard arrived. The fast key response is amazing. Even though I'm not a "gamer," I'm not a total computer novice, having gotten my first one with a gig on the hard drive--custom built--and my co-workers said I'd never use all that memory. Right. Now, my phone has 64 times that with room for expansion and does more than that first computer--or the second--and maybe more than the fifth or sixth. I still have to download the software, which will probably wait until tomorrow, but I could not wait to start playing with this. It will take me a little bit to get used to the clicking keys, but I am finding I don't mind it, after just writing this much and, well, it does bring back memories. Also, they help me notice if I don't hit a key quite hard enough. (My high school graduation present was a used Royal portable typewriter in a horrible shade of blue--and I still dislike blue. But, I think I can get past those memories. My parents weren't cheap. They were broke at that point in time.) And now, I have probably spent more on this keyboard, even though I was amazingly fortunate to be able to get this at a discount in exchange for my honest, unbiased review, than my family spent on that typewriter.

I didn't know what to expect of this, but read what one of the other reviewers said about not needing to be a gamer to appreciate this and I can see, already, how true it is. My typing is faster and I'm just using this for the first time. And now with this and the matching mouse, I can dare to try some of the things I hear about. No, even with this pair, I won't have the fast response time of someone a few decades (well, quite a few) younger, but it will sure help even the playing field for me. I am so eager to put this through its paces and I am SO happy with it, even this soon, that I have no problem recommending anything with the Bloody name on it. And I did live in New Zealand for a while, so know that Bloody is not quite considered a polite word. Too Bloody bad. I love the keyboard and mouse!

Addendum: Two days along and I'm even happier! Wow! This thing is great!

reviews.title: Keyed Up
reviews.text: I don't do a lot of gaming but this is nicer than the old Dell keyboard I was using.  It's kind of loud but otherwise I haven't had any problems typing.  The keys light up when the computer is on and during idle times it puts on a little light show.  I use the old home row technique and haven't seen any increase in errors.  Hunt and peck types might feel differently. I already have a wrist rest so I haven't used the one that attaches to the keyboard.

reviews.title: Nice keyboard even if it's not mechanical
reviews.text:
Nice keys that feel similar to mechanical. Spacebar is set up so you can press it on the edges and it will respond just as well as hitting the middle. Larger keys such as Backspace, Return, Ctrl, and the Windows all work fine.

QWER and ASDF keys are colored orange and have a unique, bumpy texture you don't feel on the smooth black keys. I would have preferred orange keys on ASDF and JKL; since that is "Home Row" for typists. The bumpy texture would make it easier to find Home Row without looking.

Arrow keys are the common one on top and three below configuration. The keys I don't often see are a FN key and a weird key with no text label instead of a second Windows key on the right of the spacebar. The "weird" key seems to work as if you right-clicked the mouse. Finally, you will see the Print Screen (PS), Scroll Lock (SL), and Page/Break (PB) keys placed above the numeric keypad.

Aside from the extra keys, the slightly increased resistance, lit keys, and the increased weight of the keyboard itself, there isn't much else that separates this from a standard keyboard. All keys stay lit when you type and switch to rotating patterns after a minute or two of inactivity. Hit any key and you are back to fully lit.

reviews.title: Solid and positive
reviews.text:
There is a lot to like about the Bloody Gaming LED B720 Light Strike Mechanical Gaming Keyboard. Fit and feel, construction, lighting scheme, and most importantly “mechanical keys” are all first rate.

Right out of the box, you can feel the weight of this keyboard. No lightweight here. Plugging it in, there are no issues with Windows 7 to 10 – drivers are automatically detected and the keyboard is immediately available. The keys light up with a rainbow of color, with each row presenting a different color. On this B720 model, there is no way to change the color scheme, but most people will find it pleasant. In idle mode, it presents a couple of animations, upon which any key touch will immediately bring back full lighting. Lighting is dimmable to totally “off”.

Of course, you do not purchase a keyboard like this without awareness that a variety of mechanical key types exist. My other keyboard is a Tt esport MEKA Cherry Brown with Cherry MX Brown switches. Cherry MX brown switches have a moderate travel distance, with some tactile feedback for keystroke and a definite bottom and light clicking noise. For me, Brown switches provide a nice blend for gaming and touch typing. The Bloody B720 has an Infrared Optic Switch exclusively developed by Bloody, and indicated to be closest to a Cherry MX Black switch. For me, comparing the Optic Switch to Cherry Brown switches, the throw distance seems shorter for the Bloody keys, with less resistance and the bottom just slightly softer. Click wise, it is mechanically positive, but noise wise less than the brown switch. Personally, I find both very desirable for the way I type and game, and struggle with finding a preference between “Optic black” and Cherry Brown.

On other aspects of the keyboard, the Bloody B720 comes with textured and differently colored gaming silicone keys, along with a nice little left hand support for same. The quick guide provides limited documentation and the Bloody website for software download. While I did not spend a lot of time with the software, it allows macros and some customization—although no changes to lighting for the B720.

If there is anything someone might not like about the Bloody B720 it would probably be the font selection for the keys (a bit sci-fi) along with an inability to control the lighting a bit more fully. For me, it is fine, I like the way it behaves during use and in its more animated modes.

Bloody Gaming may or may not be right about their own proprietary keys becoming popular, since gamers have definite preferences and a multitude of experiences with a variety of keyboards. I can say, that for the type of work and play I do on a daily basis, I would recommend this keyboard to anyone thinking about a mechanical keyboard. The Bloody B720 is an attractive solid keyboard providing a great mechanical key typing and gaming experience.

title: VELOCIFIRE Keycap Puller Tool, 3 in 1 Keycap Puller, Stainless Steel Keycap Removal Tool, Key Pulling, Loosen, Tighten Keycap Stem, Expert for Mechanical Keyboard(Black)
features: This wire style keycap puller is a necessary tool for any mechanical keyboard owner. Easily remove and replace keycaps with this keycap removal tool.
Creative two-stage combination design, multifunctional keycap puller, one part is keycap puller, one part is keycap stem adjust.
You just need to place the keycap puller metal at the bottom of the keycap and pull the keycap out vertically.
Round body shape designed for maximum comfort.Compatible for most mechanical keyboards including Cherry MX，Kailh, Outemu, and Content Switches.
Wires can bend outward to fit onto every key except the spacebar. (The spacebar key can be removed by hand after the surrounding keys are removed).

reviews.title: It's good, but it doesn't fit Daskeyboard keys
reviews.text: I have a das keyboard.  This doesn't fit over the keys.  I had to twist and bend it to make it work.  It's not the right tool for a das keyboard.  With that said, it's a good quality tool.  I would buy it again for something with smaller keys.  Just don't bother for the mechanical das keyboard.

title: YMDK Idobao x ID75 75 Keys Ortholinear Layout VIA Anodized Aluminum Case Plate hot-swappable Hot Swap Type C PCB Mechanical Keyboard Kit (Black)
description: Idobao x YMDK ID75 75 Keys Ortholinear Layout VIA Anodized Aluminum Case Plate hot-swappable Hot Swap Type C PCB Mechanical Keyboard Kit (Black)

reviews.title: Excellent keyboard - works with VIA configurator out of the box
reviews.text: Excellent build quality, just add switches & keycaps and go.  Underglow looks fantastic.  PCB supports through-hole LED switch backlighting, though obviously this would make changing switches more complicated - I'll probably try that on my next build.

title: Ganss ALT 71 Wireless/Wired 2.4G + USB Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Clear Switch for Mac and Windows, PBT Keycaps for Desktop and Laptop Computer, 71 Keys (Can Be Customize to 68 Keys Layout)
features: Cherry MX Clear Switches - Medium Tactile and Light Clicky. They are ideal for people who want to use their keyboard for both gaming and typing.

Compact and Portable - Enjoy the freedom of wirelessness with a 2.4G connection. Reliable and fast connects with devices. It saves desk space and fits right in the backpack to carry around. Perfect for home, work and on the go.
Full Keys Anti-ghosting - All 71 keys are conflict free N-Key rollover with no input error for ultimate Gaming performance, it’s more friendly to users, ensuring smooth gaming experience.
Wireless keyboard - Simply connect your portable keyboard with one USB receiver without any software.

PBT Dye Sublimation - With high-quality PBT keycaps and an anti-grease coating, these 71-key keycaps provide top-notch durability and a great feel for your typing. Advanced five-sided sublimation technology makes the pattern not fade.

reviews.title: Only the 3 switches above the arrow keys are hotswapable
reviews.text:
Ganss ALT 71 Wireless/Wired 2.4G + USB Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Brown Switch for Mac and Windows, PBT Keycaps for Desktop and Laptop Computer, 71 Keys (Can Be Customize to 68 Keys Layout)

This Keyboard is weird. I don't know why they made only the 3 switches above the arrow key hotswapable and not the others. I like the customization of the layout, but c'mon. The keyboard sounds terrible and with the limited tuning I was able to do, it sounds and feels a bit better. The wireless works fine and doesn't lose connection. The included keycaps are not great either. I replaced those straight away. If you are looking for a keyboard to mod this is not it.

reviews.title: Compact and functional at a premium price
reviews.text:
The Ganss ALT 71 keyboard is good, but not good enough to justify the price tag. I do really like that it's wireless--the dongle works well and I didn't experience any lag while typing for work or gaming. The keyboard even came with batteries, which was great! The wired option works well, too.

I didn't love the keycaps and replaced them right away with some that I already had, which are more smooth and have a broader key surface. That's a personal preference thing, though.

I've never used clear switches and was excited to give them a try. I usually use red switches, and found the clear switches to be very comfortable. They offer more resistance and shorter travel than red switches. Once I switched to lower profile key caps, I found the noise level to be slightly quieter as well. There is a bit of spring squeak when typing, though. There are also no feet on the keyboard to adjust the height/angle of your typing surface.

Only three of the switches can be removed, though I cannot figure out why you would... It removes functionality (why would you want a page up key, but not page down?). The only reason to do this that I could think of was to more distinctly isolate the arrow keys for gaming.

The keyboard came with a keycap puller and I was able to replace all of the caps without too much trouble. The keyboard is nice and compact and I do like that it's wireless. But for the price, it's very strange that there's no backlighting or truly hot-swappable switches.

reviews.title: Cherry Switches, dual USB/wireless, but missing other features
reviews.text:
I read the specifications several times for the Ganss keyboard, thinking I had missed something. Some things, in fact. The basic product -- the experience of typing on it -- is solid, and it's good to have both wireless and wired alternatives in the same keyboard.

But . . .

At this price point, you are going to do without several features that you might want. See CON.

But what you do get is a compact (no numeric pad, no function key row) keyboard whose touch I would judge to be halfway between gamer ("clacky") and laptop membrane-like (near-silent, minimal key feedback, short throw). The keys are durable, rollover-tolerant, likely top of the line at the key level.

PRO
- Compact
- Durable, with keytop replacement tool supplied
- Well-considered "middle feel" tactile design
- Status lights are below the keyboard, which makes them more visible when accidentally set (e.g., caps lock)
- Molded plastic cover will probably last awhile

CON
- Better keep the supplied manual (an annotated 2-pp diagram), as you may not be able to figure out key combinations after not using this for awhile. Function key access, for instance, is anything but intuitive
- Limited programmability
- No USB hub/auxiliary port (for mouse)
- Not backlit (requires different model from this maker)
- Tilt feature I usually use didn't work well for me YMMV
- I miss having an alternate ENTER key on the right edge somewhere
- The DEL key is oddly placed

Despite these limitations, you might like this model for the feel.

reviews.title: There are Much Better Options for the Price
reviews.text: This board was okay but at $100 it's hard to justify it's price. The build quality is similar to your run of the mill gk61 or sk61, so it's a plastic tray mount case with an some kind of metal plate not sure if it's steel or aluminum. The board isn't hotswap aside from 3 keys in the nav cluster on the right side of the board that can be covered with a plastic plate. I personally think Ganss was really brave to offer Cherry MX Clears as an option as those bottom out at nearly 100g but I'm here for it. The acoustics of the board are pretty bad this thing is loud and I would not recommend it for an office environment unless you really hate your coworkers. The stabilizers were sufficiently lubed and fit well in the plate so they didn't rattle. The keycaps seem like a cheap dye sub set and weren't terrible. The board lacks any kind of backlighting which I find strange as it has north facing switches which is usually the reason to have them that way otherwise they can interference with row 3 of cherry profile keycaps. The board has Bluetooth but doesn't have a rechargeable battery included, just 2 AAA Duracell batteries which I will give them credit for as Duracell is a good brand. Overall the board doesn't really do anything to justify it's steep price point there are much better boards in it's price range that include better switches, backlighting, and way better acoustics.

reviews.title: Compact, Well-Made
reviews.text:
This super-compact mechanical keyboard has a solid feel for a plastic keyboard at this price point. The travel and build quality of the keys feel great, and as expected for MX Clears (which are softer than other Cherry MX switches I have used). Having a detachable USB-C cable plus wireless is the best of all worlds for connectivity and long-term usability, and should be the standard for all keyboards moving forward.

I prefer a larger footprint for my daily driver, but this is a great keyboard if you need portability or work in a small space, and still want the benefits of Cherry MX switches.

n.b. The keyboard is not backlit, which increased battery life, but definitely reduces its "gamer chic" vibe.
